status_definitions.Verified: Current repository evidence directly verifies the complete requirement.
status_definitions.Partial: A relevant control exists, but the requirement is not completely proven or has a documented gap.
status_definitions.Not verified: Current evidence shows the requirement is unmet or no adequate control exists.
status_definitions.Not applicable: The application does not use the technology or flow covered by the requirement.
status_definitions.Not assessed: The requirement has not received sufficient requirement-specific review.

not_applicable_chapters.evidence: security.js and server.js use opaque server-side reference sessions; no JWT, JWS, or other self-contained authentication or authorization token is accepted
not_applicable_chapters.notes: Project-A does not implement self-contained tokens.

not_applicable_chapters.evidence: package.json, server.js, and security.js contain no OAuth 2.0 or OpenID Connect client, resource server, authorization server, or identity-provider flow
not_applicable_chapters.notes: Project-A uses first-party username/password sessions and does not implement OAuth or OIDC.

not_applicable_chapters.evidence: package.json, server.js, and public/app.js contain no WebRTC, TURN, media-server, or signaling implementation
not_applicable_chapters.notes: Project-A has no real-time audio, video, TURN, SRTP, or WebRTC signaling surface.

assessments.evidence: notifications.js endpoint allowlist; url-safety.js; test/notifications.test.js rejects untrusted push endpoints
assessments.notes: Outbound push endpoints are parsed and restricted before the server contacts them.

assessments.evidence: security.js accessCookieNames(); server.js serializeCookie()
assessments.notes: Production cookies can receive Secure, but current cookie names use palziv_* rather than the ASVS-required __Secure- or __Host- prefixes.

assessments.evidence: server.js serializeCookie() defaults SameSite=Lax; test/server-security.test.js exercises authenticated browser flows
assessments.notes: Session cookies use a same-site policy appropriate for same-origin portal navigation.

assessments.evidence: security.js accessCookieNames(); server.js serializeCookie() uses Path=/palzivalerts by default
assessments.notes: Session cookie names do not use the __Host- prefix and their application-scoped Path is incompatible with that prefix.

assessments.evidence: server.js serializeCookie() adds HttpOnly for session cookies; test/server-security.test.js authentication flows
assessments.notes: Session values are issued only through Set-Cookie and are not exposed to client JavaScript.

assessments.evidence: server.js SECURITY_HEADERS sets Strict-Transport-Security with includeSubDomains
assessments.notes: HSTS is present, but the configured max-age is 15552000 seconds rather than the ASVS minimum of one year.

assessments.evidence: server.js SECURITY_HEADERS; test/server-security.test.js baseline security header test
assessments.notes: CSP restricts scripts and styles to self and blocks objects and framing without unsafe-inline.

assessments.evidence: server.js SECURITY_HEADERS; test/server-security.test.js baseline security header test
assessments.notes: All application and static responses receive X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff.

assessments.evidence: server.js SECURITY_HEADERS; test/server-security.test.js baseline security header test
assessments.notes: Referrer-Policy is no-referrer.

assessments.evidence: server.js requireSameOrigin() and CSRF token checks; test/server-security.test.js
assessments.notes: State-changing authenticated requests require same-origin context and CSRF proof.

assessments.evidence: server.js resolveManagedDataFile(); runtime-files.js; test/runtime-files.test.js and test/server-security.test.js
assessments.notes: Runtime paths are server-controlled, constrained to the managed data directory, and reject unsafe link traversal.

assessments.evidence: security.js login throttling; docs/OPERATIONS_RUNBOOK.md; test/server-security.test.js trusted proxy throttle coverage
assessments.notes: Rate limiting is implemented and tested, but a complete anti-automation policy and malicious-lockout analysis are not documented.

assessments.evidence: security.js authentication throttle; test/server-security.test.js trusted proxy login throttling; test/security.test.js recovery lockout
assessments.notes: Authentication and recovery flows enforce bounded failure handling.

assessments.evidence: security.js TOTP implementation; server.js ADMIN_MFA_ENABLED default; test/security.test.js and test/server-security.test.js admin MFA coverage
assessments.notes: Privileged administrators use MFA by default, but employee accounts remain single-factor and no ASVS relaxation rationale exists.

assessments.evidence: security.js recovery and emergency-account flows; test/security.test.js HR recovery and MFA tests
assessments.notes: Recovery is separated and audited, but requirement-specific proof that every reset path preserves MFA is incomplete.

assessments.evidence: security.js uses node:crypto randomBytes for MFA seeds, session material, recovery, and invitation secrets
assessments.notes: Security-sensitive generated values use the platform CSPRNG.

assessments.evidence: security.js recovery attempt limiter; test/security.test.js HR recovery codes lock out after repeated guesses
assessments.notes: Code-based recovery is protected against online brute force.

assessments.evidence: security.js session lookup and authorization helpers; server.js role access gates
assessments.notes: All authoritative session verification occurs in the backend.

assessments.evidence: security.js session creation uses node:crypto randomBytes with at least 128 bits of entropy
assessments.notes: Reference session tokens use the platform CSPRNG.

assessments.evidence: security.js login and session creation; test/security.test.js and test/server-security.test.js session revocation coverage
assessments.notes: Successful authentication creates a fresh session rather than promoting an existing anonymous token.

assessments.evidence: security.js password reset and session revocation; test/server-security.test.js password reset revokes existing managed session
assessments.notes: Credential resets revoke other active sessions.

assessments.evidence: security.js MFA setup and account-update flows; no requirement-specific fresh re-authentication control or regression test found
assessments.notes: An authenticated session is required, but fresh full re-authentication before every sensitive authentication-attribute change is not proven.

assessments.evidence: ACCESS_CONTROL_LIST.md; docs/OWNER_ROLE_MODEL.md
assessments.notes: Role and function-level authorization rules are documented.

assessments.evidence: server.js resource authorization gates; test/server-security.test.js role-boundary and employee-access coverage
assessments.notes: High-risk resource boundaries are tested, but no complete endpoint-by-endpoint BOLA test inventory exists.

assessments.evidence: server.js requireHrAccess(), requireWebmasterAccess(), requireItAccess(), and employee session gates
assessments.notes: Authorization is enforced by the server and does not rely on UI visibility.

assessments.evidence: docs/security/OWASP_OPERATIONAL_CONTROLS.md secret rotation and cryptographic inventory controls; docs/SECRET_ROTATION.md
assessments.notes: Lifecycle ownership and rotation are defined, but the policy is not yet mapped to a recognized key-management standard such as NIST SP 800-57.

assessments.evidence: docs/security/OWASP_OPERATIONAL_CONTROLS.md requires a restricted cryptographic inventory; no current completed inventory artifact exists in the repository
assessments.notes: The required inventory process is documented, but execution evidence is pending.

assessments.evidence: security.js hashPassword() uses crypto.scryptSync with random salt
assessments.notes: A memory-hard KDF is used, but parameters are implicit Node defaults and are not documented against current password-storage guidance.

assessments.evidence: security.js and server.js use node:crypto randomBytes for security-sensitive tokens
assessments.notes: Reviewed secrets meet the entropy intent, but no complete cryptographic inventory proves every non-guessable value.

assessments.evidence: docs/security/OWASP_OPERATIONAL_CONTROLS.md Cloudflare TLS policy; DEPLOY_CLOUDFLARE.md
assessments.notes: TLS 1.2/1.3 is required operationally, but current external scan evidence must be captured and retained.

assessments.evidence: docs/security/OWASP_OPERATIONAL_CONTROLS.md Cloudflare TLS and origin controls; DEPLOY_CLOUDFLARE.md
assessments.notes: The public architecture requires HTTPS, but source code alone cannot prove the active edge and origin configuration.

assessments.evidence: docs/security/OWASP_OPERATIONAL_CONTROLS.md requires a publicly trusted Cloudflare certificate and external TLS evidence
assessments.notes: The control is assigned, but current certificate-chain evidence is not stored with this assessment.

assessments.evidence: server.js diagnostics authorization; test/server-security.test.js anonymous diagnostics denial
assessments.notes: No production debug mode is exposed and diagnostics require privileged access.

assessments.evidence: ACCESS_CONTROL_LIST.md; docs/OPERATIONS_RUNBOOK.md identifies sensitive runtime files and backups
assessments.notes: Sensitive stores are identified, but a complete data inventory with formal protection levels is absent.

assessments.evidence: docs/OPERATIONS_RUNBOOK.md; docs/BACKUP_AND_RESTORE.md; docs/SECRET_ROTATION.md
assessments.notes: Protection procedures exist, but encryption, retention, privacy, and logging rules are not consolidated by data class.

assessments.evidence: runtime-files.js; ACCESS_CONTROL_LIST.md; docs/OPERATIONS_RUNBOOK.md
assessments.notes: Path, atomic-write, and ACL controls exist; application-level encryption at rest is not implemented.

assessments.evidence: docs/security/OWASP_OPERATIONAL_CONTROLS.md defines dependency review and remediation deadlines by severity
assessments.notes: Critical issues are due within 72 hours, high within 7 days, moderate within 30 days, and low within 90 days.

assessments.evidence: package-lock.json; npm audit --omit=dev
assessments.notes: Dependencies are pinned and auditable, but a maintained SBOM and trusted-repository policy are not present.

assessments.evidence: server.js TRUST_PROXY_ADDRESSES validation and trustedProxyRequest(); test/server-security.test.js proxy and throttling coverage
assessments.notes: Forwarded client IP data is used only from explicitly trusted proxy addresses.

assessments.evidence: security.js and analytics.js ISO timestamps; Windows time synchronization remains an operational control
assessments.notes: Application timestamps are UTC ISO strings, but host clock synchronization needs operational evidence.

assessments.evidence: security.js security-event recording; test/security.test.js failed-login and recovery events
assessments.notes: Authentication events are recorded, but comprehensive bypass-event coverage and centralized alerting are not proven.
